description: city in Cameron County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: San Benito is a city in Cameron County, in the U.S. state of Texas, United States.

description: seat of Cameron County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Brownsville ( BROWNZ-vil) is a city in the U.S. state of Texas and the largest city and county seat of Cameron County, located on the western Gulf Coast in South Texas, adjacent to the border with Matamoros, Tamaulipas, Mexico.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: defunct training base of the United States Navy in San Diego, California, USA
description_wiki: Naval Training Center San Diego (NTC San Diego) is a former United States Navy base located at the north end of San Diego Bay, used as a training facility, commonly known as "boot camp".

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: 1919 Tennessee-class battleship
description_wiki: USS Tennessee, hull number BB-43, was the lead ship of the Tennessee class of dreadnought battleships built for the United States Navy in the 1910s.

entity: Pacific Theater of World War II

description: sea in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Philippine Sea is a marginal sea of the Western Pacific Ocean east of the Philippine Archipelago and the largest sea in the world, occupying an estimated surface area of 5 million square kilometers (2×10^6 sq mi).

description: chain of islands in the Northern Pacific
description_wiki: The Aleutian Islands (   ə-LOO-shən; Russian: Алеутские острова, romanized: Aleutskiye ostrova; Aleut: Unangam Tanangin, "land of the Aleuts"; possibly from the Chukchi aliat, or "island")—also called the Aleut Islands, Aleutic Islands, or, before the Alaska Purchase in 1867, the Catherine Archipelago—are a chain of 14 main, larger volcanic islands and 55 smaller ones.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: continent located mainly in the southwestern quadrant of the Earth
description_wiki: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere.

description: US Navy installation in San Diego, California, United States

surface_forms: National Steel and Ship Building Company
description: shipyard in San Diego, CA owned by General Dynamics
description_wiki: National Steel and Shipbuilding Company (NASSCO) is an American shipbuilding company with four shipyards located in San Diego, Norfolk, Bremerton, and Mayport.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Marty Martinez may refer to:

aliases: National Liberation Front for South Vietnam
National Front for the Liberation of South Vietnam
description: revolutionary organization active in South Vietnam and Cambodia from 1960 to 1977
description_wiki: The Viet Cong (VC) was an epithet and umbrella term to refer to the communist-led armed movement and united front organization in South Vietnam.

description: Texas, a former US Amy post
description_wiki: Fort Brown (originally Fort Texas) was a military post of the United States Army in Cameron County, Texas, during the latter half of the 19th century and the early part of the 20th century.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

aliases: 2-(2,4-dichlorophenoxy)acetic acid butyl ester;2-(2,4,5-trichlorophenoxy)acetic acid butyl ester
description: military herbicide
description_wiki: Agent Orange is a chemical herbicide and defoliant, one of the tactical uses of Rainbow Herbicides.

description: major river in Southeast Asia
country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: French commune in Indre, Centre-Val de Loire
description_wiki: Châteauroux ( SHAT-oh-ROO; French pronunciation: [ʃɑtoʁu] ; Occitan: Chasteurós) is the capital city of the French department of Indre, central France and the second-largest town in the province of Berry, after Bourges.

description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The American Legion, commonly known as the Legion, is an organization of U.S. veterans headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana.

aliases: Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States
Veterans of Foreign Wars of the U.S.
description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW), formally the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States, is an organization of United States war veterans who fought in wars, campaigns, and expeditions on foreign land, waters, or airspace as military service members.

description: non-profit organization
description_wiki: The Retired Enlisted Association (TREA) is a 501(c)(19)  non-profit organization of the United States.

description: largest city in Vietnam
